rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26's question about whether reduce_scatter test verification at test_expert_parallel_layout.py:121 is sufficient), so recall is low for both; X touches the same file/mechanism tangentially (line 24) giving it a marginal edge. X delivers five concrete, file/line-anchored nits (missing branch-coverage tests, stale comment, terse docstring) that are all grounded and directly actionable ('add this test', 'add this sentence'). Y provides a thorough but largely unfalsifiable checklist of PASS verdicts plus a single softer, prose-style comment about dp_metadata caching — solid analysis of the fix's correctness but less concrete and less actionable, and it recommends APPROVE with no comments that map to reviewer concerns. Overall X edges out on precision and actionability while both are weak on recall.
